name: Pan Fried or Sauteed Yellow Perch
recipeInstructions:
1. Dress, scale, and pat the fish dry. Salt and pepper it.
2. Dredge each fillet in a cornmeal mixture.
3. Heat the bacon fat or lard until hot enough to cook quickly but not burning. Lay the fish in the pan, reducing heat to a low medium flame.
4. Brown the fish for 8-10 minutes per side, adjusting heat if it cooks too fast. Do not overcook; the flesh should be opaque and all sides browned when done.
5. Add butter to the skillet after removing the fish. Squeeze in lemon juice (if using) and stir the fat and juices to loosen the dredging. Pour over the browned fish.
6. Variation: After removing the fish, add about 1/3 cup of chopped black walnuts, brown them lightly with remaining butter, and sprinkle over the fish before serving.
